2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new callback function called ->iomap_next() in the iomap API. This function replaces the existing ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() callbacks, allowing for more efficient iteration over mappings. The ->iomap_next() callback is responsible for finishing the previous mapping if necessary and producing the next mapping. This change enables performance-critical callers to inline their iteration loop and pass their ->iomap_next() function as a compile-time constant, which can be devirtualized by the compiler into a direct call instead of an indirect call through a function pointer. The patch also updates several files to use the new callback function.
